event: Rural ploughing scene in the English countryside
symbols: ploughing — biblical metaphor for spiritual preparation of the heart to receive the seed of God's word
vast sky with clouds — evokes divine providence and the Creator's sovereignty over the earth
open fields — the world as a field ready for sowing, echoing Christ's parable of the sower
trees at field's edge — the settled, rooted life of those who dwell on the land God provides
description: A wide panoramic landscape under a towering sky filled with luminous cream and grey clouds. In the middle distance, a man guides a plough drawn by two horses across a brown, freshly turned field. The foreground and right side are framed by clusters of dark trees, while beyond the ploughed area a green patchwork of meadows and hedgerows stretches to a far, hazy horizon. The light breaks through the clouds at center, casting a warm glow over the expansive rural scene.

scripture_refs.reason: Jesus' saying that no one who puts his hand to the plough and looks back is fit for the kingdom of God
The parable of the sower — ploughing prepares the soil that receives the seed of the word
God's provision for the earth through rain and harvest, crowning the year with bounty
The mandate to work the ground by the sweat of one's brow after the Fall
